role_summary: SNCC national office staff (1964-1966), based in Atlanta and organizing Northern student support under Executive Director James Forman.
biographical_paragraph: Betty Garman Robinson was born January 8, 1939 in Pleasantville, New York, and died October 11, 2020 [src: 1]. She was a student activist at Skidmore College in the late 1950s, where she organized campus forums with the National Student Association opposing anti-Communist redbaiting [src: 1]. In February 1960 she joined Woolworth's pickets in solidarity with the Southern sit-in movement [src: 1]. As a graduate student at the University of California, Berkeley she was a member of Students for a Democratic Society [src: 1]. In March 1964 she joined the SNCC national office in Atlanta and worked under Executive Director James Forman organizing Northern support, with responsibilities for resource allocation, fundraising, student outreach, and organizing Friends of SNCC groups [src: 1]. She collaborated with Casey Hayden, then a SNCC staff member [src: 1]. Her oral history is held by the Library of Congress as entry 7 of the Civil Rights History Project [src: 2].

sources.title: Betty Garman Robinson oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress
